title: Redesign timesheet as calendar UI and fix submission errors for all roles

requirements.text: Redesign `frontend/src/pages/employee/EmployeeTimesheetPage.tsx` with a modern calendar-style layout. Replace the plain table rows with a 7-column weekly calendar grid (Sunday–Saturday) where each day is a card showing the day name, date, start time input, end time input, Building # input, and auto-calculated paid hours. Apply the dark navy/amber theme with card elevation, subtle borders, and clean typography. The layout must feel uncluttered — use whitespace, clear section headers, and visually distinct day cards. The running total hours display and break deduction note should appear in a summary bar below the calendar. The 'Submit Timesheet' button should be prominently placed and styled. Previously submitted timesheets should appear in a clean card list below with status badges.

quotes: Change the timesheet formatting to a calendar like formatting make it cleaner better and make it not culide it's cool and nice looking and modern looking

acceptanceCriteria: The timesheet entry form displays a 7-column calendar grid (Sun–Sat) with one card per day
Each day card contains start time, end time, Building #, and auto-calculated paid hours fields
The layout is visually uncluttered with clear spacing between day cards
The dark navy/amber visual theme is applied consistently throughout the redesigned page
A summary bar below the calendar shows total paid hours for the week and the break deduction note
The Submit Timesheet button is prominently styled and accessible
Previously submitted timesheets are listed below the calendar with status badges
All existing functionality (break deduction, building number, week navigation) is preserved

requirements.text: Fix the timesheet submission error in `frontend/src/pages/employee/EmployeeTimesheetPage.tsx` and the `useSubmitTimesheet` mutation in `frontend/src/hooks/useQueries.ts` so that the employee ID is automatically derived from the authenticated session (via `AuthContext`) rather than requiring manual input or causing an error when not present. The `submitTimesheet` call must pass the `employeeId` from the session context without any user-facing prompt for it. If `employeeId` is not available in the session, display a clear inline warning ('Your account is not linked to an employee profile') instead of an unhandled error.

quotes: make it where it doesn't error when a employee try's to submit time sheet to the manager

acceptanceCriteria: Employees can submit timesheets without any prompt or field for employee ID
The employeeId is read directly from AuthContext session state and passed to the backend
If employeeId is missing from the session, a user-friendly inline warning is shown and submission is blocked
No unhandled promise rejection or console error occurs during timesheet submission
A success toast is shown on successful submission
The submitted timesheet appears in the history list with status 'Pending Manager Review'

requirements.text: Fix the error that occurs when a Manager submits a timesheet review to HR in `frontend/src/pages/manager/ManagerTimesheetPage.tsx` and the `useManagerReviewTimesheet` mutation in `frontend/src/hooks/useQueries.ts`. Investigate the 'needs employee ID' error: ensure the mutation payload includes all required fields (timesheet ID, approved boolean, comment) and does not inadvertently require or mispass an employee ID. Ensure the mutation correctly maps `#ok`/`#err` backend response variants to resolved/rejected Promise states and that the actor is non-null before the call is made.

quotes: when manager submits to HR saying it errors and says need employee ID make it work when a employee is just login to there account

acceptanceCriteria: Managers can approve or deny submitted timesheets without receiving an 'employee ID' error
The useManagerReviewTimesheet mutation sends timesheet ID, approved flag, and comment only — no extra employee ID field required
The mutation guards against null/undefined actor before calling the backend
Backend #ok/#err variants are correctly mapped to resolved/rejected Promise states
A success toast is shown after manager approves or denies a timesheet
The reviewed timesheet moves from 'Pending Your Review' to 'Reviewed / Processed' immediately after action

requirements.text: Audit and fix the backend `submitTimesheet` endpoint in `backend/main.mo` to accept and store the `employeeId` from the timesheet record payload rather than requiring it to be derived from a caller principal that may not be linked. Ensure the `managerReviewTimesheet` endpoint does not require an `employeeId` parameter beyond the timesheet ID, approved boolean, and optional comment. Verify that both endpoints return well-formed `#ok`/`#err` variants and do not trap on missing optional fields.

quotes: it doesn't error when a employee try's to submit time sheet to the manager
when manager submits to HR saying it errors and says need employee ID

acceptanceCriteria: submitTimesheet accepts employeeId as part of the timesheet payload and stores it without error
managerReviewTimesheet accepts only timesheetId, approved (Bool), and optional comment — no separate employeeId parameter
Both endpoints return #ok on success and #err with a descriptive message on failure
Neither endpoint traps or panics on valid input
Existing timesheet records remain queryable after the fix

constraints: Do not modify any files under frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts, frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts, frontend/src/main.tsx, or frontend/src/components/ui
Preserve all existing timesheet functionality: 30-minute break deduction, Building # field, Sunday-first week, week navigation, and status history list
Maintain the dark navy/amber visual theme throughout the redesigned timesheet page
nonGoals: Redesigning the Manager or HR timesheet review pages (only fixing their submission errors)
Changing the two-stage approval workflow logic
Adding new timesheet fields beyond what already exists
